headline: European bison return to Romania after 200 years
meta_description: Over 250 European bison now roam freely in Romania's Southern Carpathians, more than 200 years after disappearing from the region.
summary_short: European bison have made a remarkable comeback in Romania's Southern Carpathians. More than 250 animals now roam wild, with nearly half born in nature.
body:
European bison have returned to Romania's Southern Carpathians after vanishing from the region for more than 200 years. Over 250 of the massive animals now roam freely across the landscape, marking a quiet but significant recovery for a species once hunted to extinction across much of Europe.

What makes this resurgence particularly encouraging is the role of natural reproduction. Nearly half of the current population was born in the wild—a sign that the animals are not merely surviving in a managed reintroduction program, but genuinely establishing themselves and breeding on their own. This suggests the ecosystem is healthy enough to sustain them without constant human intervention.

The restoration reflects decades of conservation effort across Europe. The Southern Carpathians, with their vast forests and rugged terrain, have proven an ideal sanctuary for these forest dwellers to rebuild their numbers and reclaim habitat their ancestors inhabited centuries ago.
quick_facts: Over 250 European bison now live in Southern Carpathians
Species absent from Romania for more than 200 years
Nearly half of current population born in the wild
